unit.name: Causal masking and the minimal self-attention architecture
unit.description: Explains how to prevent information leakage from future tokens and assembles the minimal usable self-attention block.
unit.summary: For language modeling and decoder-side sequence generation, self-attention cannot look ahead to future tokens, so the lecture introduces causal masking by setting scores for future positions to negative infinity before softmax, which forces their attention weights to zero [ts=1999s] [ts=2023s]. The instructor illustrates this with a lower-triangular attention pattern where the representation of "chef" can see only start/the/chef and not future words like "who," preventing training-time cheating when predicting the next token [ts=2078s] [ts=2100s]. With masking added to positional embeddings, self-attention, and a feed-forward network, the lecture defines a minimal self-attention architecture that can replace recurrence and be stacked into larger models [ts=2359s] [ts=2408s] [ts=2435s].

key_points.text: In autoregressive settings, future tokens must be masked so a token cannot use information from words that come later.
Causal masking is implemented by replacing future attention scores with negative infinity before the softmax.
After softmax, masked future positions receive weight zero in the weighted sum.
The lower-triangular visibility pattern lets "chef" attend only to earlier or current tokens, not to "who."
A minimal usable block consists of position embeddings, self-attention, masking when needed, and a feed-forward network.

unit_summary_for_generation: For language modeling and decoder-side sequence generation, self-attention cannot look ahead to future tokens, so the lecture introduces causal masking by setting scores for future positions to negative infinity before softmax, which forces their attention weights to zero [ts=1999s] [ts=2023s]. The instructor illustrates this with a lower-triangular attention pattern where the representation of "chef" can see only start/the/chef and not future words like "who," preventing training-time cheating when predicting the next token [ts=2078s] [ts=2100s]. With masking added to positional embeddings, self-attention, and a feed-forward network, the lecture defines a minimal self-attention architecture that can replace recurrence and be stacked into larger models [ts=2359s] [ts=2408s] [ts=2435s].

kp_catalog.name: Causal masking and masked self-attention
kp_catalog.description: Masking blocks selected attention links by forcing their pre-softmax scores to negative infinity, which is essential for autoregressive language modeling. Future positions are masked in decoder self-attention so next-token prediction cannot cheat by reading words that come later in the sequence.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because causal masking and masked self-attention clarifies masking blocks selected attention links by forcing their pre-softmax scores to negative infinity, which is essential for autoregressive language modeling, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.

kp_catalog.name: Self-attention
kp_catalog.description: Self-attention applies attention within one set of vectors by projecting each input into a query, key, and value so every element can mix information from the whole set. Each token can gather information from every token in the sequence in a single parallel operation, rather than relying on stepwise recurrence.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because self-attention formalizes how self-attention applies attention within one set of vectors by projecting each input into a query, key, and value so every element can mix information from the whole set, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context.
